title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible encryption and key-authorization contract with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in encryption and key-authorization contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
Order the Key Rotation Revocation Drill S3, CloudFront, and Go storage-delivery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Key Rotation Revocation Drill assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 3800 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Key Rotation Revocation Drill has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Key Rotation Revocation Drill precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 3800 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Rotating a KMS key immediately re-encrypts every existing object and revokes every URL.

options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill case

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the key rotation revocation drill · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For key rotation revocation drill · fault isolation, run one convenient Key Rotation Revocation Drill happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-key-rotation-revocation-drill-1bdwju" that uses case 8934 to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Key Rotation Revocation Drill into observable performance evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Key Rotation Revocation Drill precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 5178 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Rotating a KMS key immediately re-encrypts every existing object and revokes every URL.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Key Rotation Revocation Drill: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 2131 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Key Rotation Revocation Drill, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.
